teamObjective.explanation: Deterministically repaired ToM trace with objective protect_partner.

partnerBelief.summary: Partner (player 2) has only 2 cards left and is close to finishing. With no public cards played, the partner likely wants low-cost opportunities to shed cards or win a trick cheaply. A low single lead gives the partner the widest possible window to play a winning low card or pass the initiative without forcing them to spend a scarce high card.

opponentBelief.summary: Opponents (players 1 and 3) each hold 3 cards. They want to prevent the partner from exiting cheaply and may be willing to spend a moderately high non-trump or trump to win the opening trick. Because no trumps or big cards have been played, they are likely to have some cards above diamond-6. A low lead avoids giving the opponents a free high-card trade; if they win, they win a cheap trick.

actionRationale.primaryReason: Opening lead should be the lowest safe single (diamond-3) to preserve hand strength and help the near-finish partner escape or win cheaply.

risks: Schema repair may preserve imperfect reasoning from the raw model output.
riskAssessment.mitigation: Treat the repaired trace as schema-normalized reasoning; do not add hidden-card facts beyond the raw output.
